desc:
You stand on a relatively narrow, but grassy plateau. The wind is strong here,
blowing wildly in one direction and then abruptly changing directions. To the
west the plateau goes on for a bit before it ends.
name: A Green Plateau

desc:
Numerous types of trees and bushes grow on this lush plateau. The high altitude
provides an astonishing view of the surrounding country-side, and to the west, a
narrow, winding path leads back down to the plains below.
name: Top of a Hill

desc:
This would seem to be a gathering place for some sort of ritual. Small white
stones are placed in a perfect circle, in the centre of which stands a statue.
Who uses this place, and for what purpose, can only be imagined. The slope to
the south is slightly hollowed, and to the west lies an unpleasant field.
name: A Circle of Stones

desc:
This farmhouse is simple and rustic. It is lacking in maintenance though. The
planks in the floor have faded in colour and are beginning to rot. The furniture
consists of a few wooden chairs, a table, and some shelves on the walls.
name: An Old Farmhouse

desc:
The shrubs here grow thick and tall, making progress quite difficult. To the
east, the foliage is far too dense to force your way through, but to the west it
seems to thin out somewhat. To the south, whilst the foliage is denser than
ever, it does seem to become thinner, right at ground level.
name: Among the Hedgerow

desc:
A large, but simple, archway stands here. This must once have been a memorial of
some sort, but now all, except the arch itself, stands in ruins. Huge piles of
dust and shattered shards or stone lie all around, and the hedgerow to the south
looks wild and overgrown.
name: A Stone Archway

desc:
A pile of rubble and dust has collected here, at the bottom of the slope, and a
few large boulders lie here. Perhaps a path once passed this way, for a large
and unnatural archway stands to the south, but a landslide has made it quite
impossible to follow the trail back to the north.
name: The Bottom of a Rocky Slope

desc:
This slope seems to be far too steep and treacherous to travel upon, and it is
quite impossible to scramble back up it, to the north. Many small rocks and
stones roll down the hillside, underfoot, making it almost impossible to keep a
steady footing, even without trying to attain the summit of the hill.
name: A Rocky Slope

desc:
This cemetery is in bad need of repair. Weeds sprout up next to tombstones and
the small wooden fence which runs along the perimeter of the graveyard is
rotting and falling down. To the south, there seems to have been a landslide at
some time past, and the scree-covered slope appears to be quite steep.
name: An Ancient Graveyard

desc:
Cold stone walls and a stony ceiling do not make this a cosy place, and yet this
is evidently a living chamber of some sort. A large bed stands against one wall,
and in the centre of the room is a stout table, with five sturdy chairs around
it. A few rough straw pallets are also piled in one corner.
name: A Living Chamber

desc:
The corridor leads to the east and north here, though eastward it seems to come
to an abrupt end after only a few strides. A small pile of bones leans against
the southern wall, and above them an old, rusted pair of iron fetters hangs,
ominously.
name: A Bend in the Corridor

desc:
The hallway ends rather suddenly here. Someone has recently left a large pile of
rubbish here; scattered about the room are broken cups, fragments of wood, torn
shreds of cloth and pieces of stone. The wooden beams supporting the ceiling
appear to penetrate the eastern wall.
name: End of the Corridor

desc:
All about the room are empty crates and jugs. This seems to be a storage room of
some kind. On the floor, along the walls, there is a large number of rat
droppings. Above, it seems that one of the rafters looks different from the
others.
name: A Hidden Chamber

desc:
The stone floor is hard and cool. From here, the corridor seems to extend
forever to the north and the south. The rafters above cast shadows, obscuring
the roof. Your footsteps echo resoundingly throughout the hallway.
name: A Stone Corridor

desc:
This cool, dry intersection has a musty odour to it. It consists of a series of
cut bricks of stones placed next to each other. Scattered among them on the
floor are fragments of rusted iron chains. The corridor bends here from the
south to the east.
name: An Ancient Dungeon

desc:
The hallway comes to an abrupt end here. Judging from the iron doors on the
north, south, and east walls, this appears to have once been a prison; who used
it, and for what purpose, cannot be determined from here.
name: An Old Prison

desc:
Fungus grows on the walls of this unpleasant room, and the floor is covered by
dirt and waste. The air hangs heavy with an unpleasant odour of death and decay,
and no fresh breeze penetrates this place, to offer relief. The only apparent
exit is the heavy iron door to the west.
name: An Old Prison Cell

desc:
The walls of this room tell a horrible story. The iron door has many scratches
on it and the walls are stained red-brown from old caked blood. Some coarse
straw has been left in one corner, perhaps to serve as a bed or even a latrine,
and on one wall small scrapings in the stone appear to be an attempt to write a
message.
name: A Prison Cell

desc:
The land rolls along in an endless sea of grass, with the wind lightly brushing
the tips of the grass blades. A small column of smoke can be seen rising from
the northwest. Some trees along the north mark the field of grass, and more
woods can be seen in the distance.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
Many dead and burnt out trees stand or lie here, their brown, blackish colour of
death matching that of the grass around them. A recent rain has turned parts of
the ground to a dark red-brown mud slurry, and a huge pile of dead logs blocks
the way north and east. To the west stands an old wooden barn.
name: Worn Field

desc:
Green soft grass can be seen in the whole area, some small trees and bushes are
also spread around it. There are small holes in the ground, too small to enter
but big enough for a small animal. The country around looks a bit empty and to
the south the road to Tharbad goes in a west-east direction.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
The blue waters flow slowly and peacefully here, the wide but shallow river
bordered by banks full of flowers and bushes. In places, rocks peep out from
beneath the surface of the water, forcing it to foam and splash as it makes its
way slowly eastward, and the entire riverbed here is comprised of small stones,
in all shades of grey and black.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
The Nen Kalthar flows over many rocks and stones here, splishing and splashing
as it makes its way eastward across the countryside. A gentle breeze blows from
the east, cutting up the surface of the water even where the rocks do not make
it foam.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
The river bends here, flowing round the deep roots of a tall, old tree, which
you can see to your east. Pebbles bump about the bottom of the river, bouncing
against rocks, jumping happily away, and continuing to follow the flow of the
river, which bends south then east again.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
Gurgling contentedly, the river bends gently around the roots of a solitary tree
which stands just north-east of here, drinking a deep draught of the wholesome
waters of the Nen Kalthar. The river flows again to the east, carrying you
towards the City of Thieves.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
The river here is bordered to the north by high banks, apparently built up by
the hand of man to protect the road to the north from flooding. To the south
however, a large, square building stands low on the riverbank. It is apparent
that this structure is a mill, though whether it is still in service is not
possible to tell from here.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
Grassy fields border along the road to Tharbad towards the north, creating an
endless sea of light green blades. The soft sounds of a river can be heard
nearby, its bank sloping slightly towards the south. The grass around here is a
nice shade of dark green, a contrast to its kin in the fields.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
You are walking along a well-tended road, which borders the Nen Kalthar. You can
hear the sounds of happy birds singing nearby, and you feel untroubled by your
journey. The road runs due east-west here.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
You are walking along a well-tended road, with the Nen Kalthar drifting past to
the south. Birds are singing happily from the trees alongside the road, and all
your cares seem to melt away to nothing. The road continues to the east and
west.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
The wide roadway seems to be quite well tended in this part of the country,
probably owing to the number of farms in the area. Just south of the road, at
the bottom of an extremely steep and muddy bank, the river Nen Kalthar flows
lazily from the west.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
The road is raised slightly above the level of the surrounding countryside, on a
causeway. To the north, you can join a small path, which leads to a barn, in the
northwest, or you could follow the path further north, towards a small
farmhouse. Directly to the south stands a large, solitary tree, and the road
sweeps on past this, heading from east to west.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
The road bends here, curving gracefully from the south to the west. In the
north, a thin forest can be seen, and nestling, almost in its eaves, is a small
farmhouse. To the southwest, a solitary tree stands, as if sundered from its kin
by this wide roadway.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
A solitary tree stands here, isolated from its fellows in the north by the line
of the road to Tharbad. On the bole of the tree there is an elaborate carving
which needs a closer inspection to be seen properly. To the west, the river Nen
Kalthar flows by, diverted from its eastward course by the sturdy roots of this
tree, which make the riverbank firm and strong here.
name: A Lonely Tree

desc:
The road here is raised slightly, on a broad earthen causeway. To the south
there is a gentle slope down to a low wooden bridge, which spans the waters of
the Nen Kalthar. The road itself curves around a solitary tree, which stands on
the bank of the river, just to the west.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
The road here runs east-west, straight as an arrow. In the north you can hear
the noise of birds singing happily in the trees, and you fancy that you can
actually hear the trees sighing with contentment. Looking south, you can see the
sparkling, clear waters of the Nen Kalthar, flowing lazily toward the east.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
The noise of birdsong drifts on the air from the north, where a thin forest
stands, separated from the road here by thick, thorny undergrowth. To the south,
a small wooden building can be seen, standing right on the bank of the Nen
Kalthar, and the musical murmur of flowing water can be heard even from the
road.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
The road here bends from the south to the west, running parallel to the light
forest in the north. The roadway itself is built on a raised earthen causeway,
and at the bottom of the slope, under the boughs of the trees, there is a thick
underbrush, which prevents entry into the woodland from this location.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
This wide road roughly traces the line of the Nen Kalthar, which flows from the
west, toward the city of thieves. Here, a small hut stands beside the road,
right on the banks of the river, and to the north, the roadway is bordered by a
light forest.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
The road here follows the route chosen by the Nen Kalthar, which meanders lazily
from the west, toward Tharbad, in the southeast, where it meets the mighty River
Greyflood. To the north, a small, stony path leads into the outskirts of a thin
forest, whose trees seem alive with birdsong.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
The road bends here, curving from the north to the east. Directly east you can
see a ford, which crosses a small, but fast-flowing tributary, before it joins
with the Nen Kalthar. Far north you can see a thin forest, with trees which do
not grow very close together.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
You have come to a well-built ford. Layers of rock have been placed here, to
provide safe passage across a wide and fast-flowing stream. Although a few reeds
have grown through the rocky base, the footing is still firm and only slightly
slippery. The waters of the smaller tributary race and chase across the flat
stones, then tumble over a slight drop, to meet the larger river to the south.
Eastward the road climbs a slight rise, away from the ford.
name: A Ford on the Way to Tharbad

desc:
The road runs east to west here, along the edge of a grassy ridge. Standing by
the roadside, you can see a treacherous drop to the south that plunges down into
a steep gully. North, a muddy trail runs along the edge of a small river.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
Standing atop the brow of the hill, the road stretches west into the distance as
it follows the contours of the river. Looking south, the road starts on a gentle
downhill turn, flattening out as it approaches the bend at the bottom. Just
north from here, a pleasant glade provides an ideal resting-place.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
To the north stands a well-built but ancient ford, across which many generations
of men must have passed. Here, a wide, fast-flowing stream joins the waters of
the Nen Kalthar, as it makes its way east across the countryside.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
A short incline leads north towards a blind summit on the road, bordered by the
trees on either side. Looking east, the road levels out as it winds into the
distance. South of the road, a short drop leads down to the river's edge.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
The road here is pitted with holes and cracks in the soft ground, most likely
due to the heavy coaches which frequently traverse the turnpike. A narrow trail
departs the road to the east, heading off into the grassy fields. To the north a
light forest shadows the road as it bends around to the west.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
A rickety wooden bridge crosses the river here. Built low and almost flat, this
structure is so close to the surface of the water that in a heavy rain, a boat
might be unable to pass beneath it, and indeed the entire construct could easily
be washed away. With no rail, and much rot, the safety of this bridge must be
open to question.
name: A Small Bridge over the Nen Kalthar

desc:
Southwest of here, a large square building can be seen, standing on the bank of
the river. Just to the east, the course of the waterway has been diverted by the
construction of a small, wooden boathouse, which forces the water to turn south,
before regaining its eastward path.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
West of here a large flat rock offers a low and easy landing place for small
boats, and leads towards a large square building, which stands on the bank of
the river. A small bridge leads from the road to this landing, suggesting that
the building once saw trade of some sort, but now the area appears to be
deserted.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
A wooden jetty projects onto the Nen Kalthar from the boathouse which stands
just to the north. This old wooden construction has clearly seen better days, as
is apparent from the rotting timbers, which occasionally break free, and float
into the water here.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
The river bends here, but it is not a natural bend. Rather, it is a curve made
by men, engineered so that the banks will still hold firm, and resist erosion,
so that the road to the east of here does not become flooded during heavy rains
or other periods of high waters.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
The stony path makes a bend to the west here. It is a nice area around and the
grass is wealthy and well grown. The bushes on the side of the path are small
and with nice green leaves upon them. The sound of running water can be heard,
it comes from the north, from the Nen Kalthar. The small bridge is to the north
of here.
name: A Stony Path

desc:
The stony path makes a sharp bend here, continuing to the east and the south.
Next to the path, the vegetation grows thick and lush, with many different
flowers growing amid the long grass. The pathway itself is comprised of large
flat stones, which were perhaps taken from the bed of the Nen Kalthar, which
flows lazily through the countryside, just north of here.
name: A Stony Path

desc:
Thick bushes border the path to the east. High and thorny, they make travel in
that direction quite impossible. A clump of trees stands to the south, where a
small trail leads through a gap in the hedgerow, whilst the main path bends from
north to west here. Far to the east, a mill can be seen.
name: A Stony Path

desc:
The path travels here through a small and peaceful glade, whose tall trees would
offer welcome shade on a hot and sunny day. To the west the woodland becomes
more dense, and thick undergrowth prevents you from leaving the path to travel
in that direction. To the east, however, a gap in the tall hedgerow offers
access to a well-tended garden.
name: Path through a Peaceful Glade

desc:
The interior of this small shed is dusty, and cobwebs seem to fill every nook
and cranny. The large windows, set high in each wall, are so dirty that they let
in no natural light, and the small landing to the south is built from timbers
now so rotten that it is a wonder the jetty can still remain intact.
name: The Boat House

desc:
The banks of the river rise high here, to either side, confining it to a course
chosen not by nature, but by the hand of man. Here the waters of the Nen Kalthar
flow from the north, to the east, but to the south a small rocky creek diverts
some of the waters into the rolling hills.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
The river here is confined by steep earthen banks, altering its natural course
so that the roadway to the north might be pretected from rising waters in a
flood. To the northeast, a well-built, yet ancient ford offers travellers a
route across the fast-flowing tributary, which joins the Nen Kalthar just east
of here.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
The river bends around from the west to south here, where it flows into a steep
walled gully. The deep, dark water carries you along with some vigour and it
takes all you effort to avoid being tossed against the rockface.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
A neat path of small stones makes it easy to travel here. To the north and
south, beyond tall hedges, endless plains, covered with thick green grass, can
be seen, and the hedgerow itself is made up of thick bushes, with soft green
leaves and tiny red berries. The path stretches east and west.
name: A Stony Path

desc:
The size of this garden never ceases to amaze. Nor does the wide variety of
flora growing here quite contentedly. Surely the person in charge of this garden
is a master of his craft, for no other could coax plants from all the different
areas of Arda to grow together in perfect complement to each other. The hedge
bounds the garden on the north and east, so you can only follow the path to the
south and west.
name: A Path in a Well-tended Garden

desc:
You look around you, and everywhere you look all you can see is trees, flowers
and shrubs of many different sizes shapes and colours. This garden is surely one
of the most beautiful you are ever likely to see. The soil looks rich, dark and
moist, willing to lend succour to any plant which may be placed here. There is a
marked path leading through the garden, and you should be careful not to stray
from it, so as not to anger anyone by trampling the plants.
name: A Path in a Well-tended Garden

desc:
In the middle of the north wall of the room stands a large waterwheel, made of
aged oak, with fourteen hefty spokes. Flour has gathered in the corners and
along the walls, and also in small gaps in the floorboards. Close to the west
wall, a wooden stairway once led to the upper level of the mill, but this has
long since decayed beyond use.
name: An Old Mill

desc:
A large, flat rock makes a stable landing place and a useful roadway to the
mill, which stands just to the west of here. To the north, a small, rickety
bridge links this area to the roadway, which cuts from east to west across the
land. Beyond the road, a small farm can be seen far in the distance, nestling in
the eaves of a large woodland.
name: On a Rock

desc:
A large flat rock protrudes from the riverbank just south of here, forcing the
waters to continue eastward, though a little further downstream they turn again
to the south. A small, wooden bridge spans the river here, reaching from the
wide road to the north, toward the rocky prominence, barely rising high enough
above the waters to permit passage of even a small boat.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
A stout, but narrow bridge, made of smooth blue-grey stones, arches smoothly
above your head here, spanning the river. Grey-green moss peppers the surface of
the stones, which have been dampened by the splashing water, as the Nen Kalthar
progresses eastward, toward the city of Tharbad.
name: Beneath a Stone Bridge

desc:
The air has been standing still in here for ages and smells rotten. This room is
very small and dust lies towards the walls and all over the floor. A wooden
bench stands towards the southern wall, one of its sides has broken and it has
only 3 legs left. A small square hole can be seen in the ceiling but it is not
big enough to light up the attic.
name: In a Dusty Attic

desc:
The river here is flanked by a forest to the west, but the bank is so steep that
it would seem impossible to scale. To the east the bank is a little lower, but
still appears to be quite steep, muddy and slippery. Apparently the only choice
is to follow the fast-flowing waters, which bubble merrily toward the Nen
Kalthar, to the south.
name: A Fast-flowing Stream

desc:
Rising high around you, the earthen banks of the river afford little view of the
land about, but you can see atop the bank in the west a thin forest, from whence
comes the sounds of many birds singing, and of animals scuffling about in the
undergrowth. The water flows rapidly to the south here, and it is no easy feat
to beat the current, despite the fact that this is such a small and seemingly
insignificant tributary.
name: A Fast-flowing Stream

desc:
The stream is decidedly rough here as it tumbles forth from a very rocky and
narrow ravine to the north, and flows swiftly onward to the south, with a
violence which belies its relatively small size. Indeed travel further up-
stream would seem to be made impossible by the angry waters, and the origins of
this waterway would seem to have to remain enshrouded in mystery. To the west,
atop a steep bank, the trees of a thin forest look down upon the water- way,
whilst to the east the bank appears too steep and muddy to scale, and its height
obscures any view of what lies beyond.
name: A Fast-flowing Stream

desc:
The pathway, upon which you tread, stands amid the eaves of an old woodland.
Soft and grassy underfoot, the trail leads from the Road to Tharbad, which lies
just south of here, to the north, deeper into the forest. From somewhere to the
east the sound of fast-running water can be heard.
name: On the Fringes of the Light Forest

desc:
Soft grass, springy underfoot, makes this pathway through the woodland. Here the
trail diverges, heading westward, or to the north, winding off between the tall
trees. To the south, a wider path leads out of the forest, toward the Road to
Tharbad, whilst from somewhere to the east, the sound of fast-running water can
be heard.
name: At an Intersection of Paths

desc:
Leaves and small twigs flutter down beside you, dislodged from up above by the
many animals and birds who make this forest their home. The grass under your
feet smells fresh, and feels damp, as though still wet with the first dew of the
morning. The trail you are on leads under arched boughs to the north and south,
or you can enter a large clearing in the middle of the forest to the west.
name: A Pleasant Trail in the Light Forest

desc:
Right in the middle of the forest there is a small ruin. It is hard to tell what
it was before, but it was something made of stone. It was probably a small house
with four rooms from what remains of the walls. There is a stair going down to
something that could have been a cellar. Near the ruin, the trees are old or
dead. They are pale and grey, and have no leaves on them.
name: At a Small Ruin

desc:
You are walking along the forest floor, under an archway high above, formed from
interlaced branches. Leaves rustle softly all around, and small animals scamper
into the undergrowth just ahead of you. The pathway continues through the trees,
both east and west, whilst to the north a large clearing opens up.
name: A Pleasant Trail in the Light Forest

desc:
The trees here form an almost perfect circle around this clearing. Trails lead
out from here in all directions, passing under the stout green boughs of the
ancient trees. In the centre of the clearing, a few small mushrooms grow, and
all around, small animals scamper away, almost unseen, at the edge of your
vision.
name: A Clearing in the Forest

desc:
The trail you are on leads east and west here, or you can enter a clearing in
the middle of the forest to the south. Overhead you can hear the flapping of
wings and the scratching noises of many small feet trying to gain purchase on
the bark of the trees, as animals of the forest hurry about, packing as much
happiness and industry into their short life spans as they can possibly manage.
name: A Pleasant Trail in the Light Forest

desc:
The sound of birdsong is to be heard in the branches far above your head, whilst
at your feet small animals scurry, unseen among the undergrowth which partially
obscures the pathway here. The woodland stretches in all directions, though, to
the south, through some thick bushes, a glimpse of the road to Tharbad can be
seen.
name: A Pleasant Trail in the Light Forest

desc:
The grassy path is springy underfoot, and the sound of birdsong fills the air.
The trees rustle in the wind, and all around are signs of abundant wildlife,
which must fill this forest. To the east, a large clearing opens out before you,
whilst westward, through the trees and bushes, it is possible to glimpse some
well tended pasture fields.
name: A Pleasant Trail in the Light Forest

desc:
The trail seems to peter out here, as the trees grow closer together near the
edge of the forest, almost as though someone has deliberately planted them so
close together, to make a barrier. Through the thick undergrowth, a small
building can be seen to the west, with dark smoke curling out of its chimney,
but it is impossible to reach it, due to the dense vegetation.
name: At the End of the Trail

desc:
The smell of freshly baked bread fills this small and cosy little home. A sturdy
wooden table, surrounded by six simple chairs, stands at the centre of the room,
and cheerful curtains cover the windows. The floor is made of stout boards, and
the walls are painted neatly with bright white paint.
name: Inside a Small Farmhouse

desc:
You are standing at the edge of the fields here, but the grass is still as thick
as before. The fields continue to the west, but to the east, the thick
undergrowth of a small forest blocks your way. To the south, a grassy path leads
towards the forest.
name: A Pasture Field

desc:
The grass springs under your step, making your walk through this forest quite an
enjoyable one. Birds sing sweet songs to serenade you, and little animals scurry
here and there, at the edge of your vision. The trail leads north from here,
into an open field, or east, deeper into the woodland.
name: A Pleasant Trail in the Light Forest

desc:
You are standing in the middle of some lush fields, a sea of lush, green grass
which makes an ideal pastureland for cattle. The fields continue to the north,
where a small farmhouse can be seen, dark smoke curling from its chimney, and to
the east, where they draw close to the borders of a thin forest. Westward, a
small path leads through the fields, toward a huge wooden barn.
name: A Pasture Field

desc:
You are standing here in the middle of some fields, surrounded by grass which is
thick green and healthy - ideal pastureland. The fields stretch into the
distance, both to the south and to the west, whilst to the east a small and
simple farmhouse stands.
name: A Pasture Field

desc:
You are standing in the middle of some pastures, which stretch eastward from
here, leading eventually to a simple farmhouse, beyond which a thin forest can
be seen. To the south, a small pathway leads toward the Road to Tharbad, which
runs east-west in this part of the country. Birds occassionally fly by,
overhead, chirping and singing happily.
name: A Pasture Field

desc:
This section of the path is fairly barren, as it seems to be a fairly often-
used intersection, and the passage of many men and beasts has made the path
dusty and worn. To the south you can see a road leading east-west, while fields
extend to the north and east. To the west the dirt path continues, leading
towards a barn in the northwest, but you can also see the rooftop of a house in
the opposite direction, across the fields.
name: On a Small Path

desc:
You stand on a packed-earth path that runs from the west to the east. Directly
north of here there is a huge wooden barn which, although simply designed, looks
clean and freshly painted. From inside you can hear the sounds of peaceful
clucking, scritching, scratching and flapping. The path makes a bend in the
west, or runs east to join an intersection.
name: On a Dirt Path in Front of a Barn

desc:
The earthy path bends here around the corner of the barn, curving from the east
to the north. An odd, and not necessarily pleasant smell drifts from that
direction, but for now, you are quite unable to place its source. To the south,
a thin ribbon of road can be seen through the trees.
name: A Turn in the Dirt Path

desc:
You are standing at the end of the path, behind a large barn. A wheelbarrow is
parked here, filled with fresh manure, which smells fairly strongly, and from
inside the barn you can hear some clucking noises, along with scratching and
flapping.
name: Behind the Barn

desc:
You are standing in a neat barn, with freshly-dried hay for the horses sitting
in the feed-boxes, and several hen roosts placed along the walls. There are
stalls for cows here, lined with clean straw, but the stalls are empty right
now. The smell in here is distinct in its nature, but it is a wholesome smell,
not a sickly one.
name: Inside the Barn

desc:
Thick grasses grow in abundance all over the area giving room for very little
other vegetation. A small worn trail makes its way towards the west and south.
The land seems endlessly flat and covered with grass in all directions except to
the southwest where a building can be discerned.
name: Fields near a Tavern

desc:
Thin, dry grass carpets the ground. The carpet is only broken by large patches
of white thorn and other bushes. These bushes grow into brush to the east, and
the grass grows shorter towards the south. To the west and north, the dry land
remains the same. Very few animals inhabit this barren and desolate area.
name: Dry Land

desc:
The dry ground supports only grass and stiff coarse bushes with razor-sharp
leaves. The grass is getting shorter towards the south while the bushes get more
rare towards the west. Upon closer inspection the bushes turn out to be mainly
white thorn but also some brambles.
name: Dry Land

desc:
A dense carpet of grass covers the ground in all directions almost unbroken by
other vegetation. There is some low shrubbery towards the east. The landscape
has not been touched by civilised hands though it would make great grazing
grounds for domesticated animals like cows. There is a hint of a trail to the
west.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
A trail makes its way from west to east, branching to the south where a large
building can be seen. Long grass covers the ground in all directions north of
the trail, with little other vegetation growing there. The trail is worn,
showing signs of being used often by travellers to and from the building.
name: Trail beside a Tavern

desc:
The grass grows as high as a mans waist and totally dominates the area leaving
no room for other plant life to thrive. The grassy plains go on towards the
north while a small trail can be seen to the east. Glancing south and west the
familiar lines of the Road to Tharbad can be discerned.
name: Grasslands

desc:
The plains seem to stretch on forever in all directions with only the odd bush
to break the monotony of the grass. A gentle breeze from the north carries a
sweet scent in the air and the faint sound of songbirds can be heard in the
distance. This would make a perfect grazing ground for cattle.
name: Open Grassland

desc:
The long grass dominates the open landscape looking towards the east and north.
No other plant life has been able to gain space to grow. To the south and to the
west a big roadway can be seen and it is possible to enter it from here.
name: Plains by the Road

desc:
Long lush grass grows everywhere making the few rough bushes look like small
islands in a grassy ocean. Panning the horizon the grass seems to go on forever
like this in all directions. Looking south there seem to be a small trail
cutting a line through the long grass.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
Long grass covers the ground in all directions with a few bushes as the only
other vegetation. There is no place to take cover if the sky was to open its
floodgates. A small trail cuts its way through the grass towards the south.
Besides that, there is no hint of human presence.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
A few bushes grow in small clumps all over the land, but several kinds of tall
grasses grow in abundance, making the ground appear as if it is covered with a
thick, green carpet. East, the ground becomes drier, and the taller vegetation
turns more dominant as the lack of moisture prohibits the grass from thriving.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The dry ground supports mainly thin coarse grass and patches of bushes. Looking
around the horizon the landscape is similar in all directions save west where
the bushes are more scarce and the grass more dominant possibly due to more
nourishment in the soil.
name: Dry Land

desc:
Only thin coarse grass and patches of bushes have survived on this dry ground.
This dreary land extends to the west and south while the shrubbery grows into
brush to the east and north. Not many animals can be found here since the land
offers so little nourishment.
name: Dry Land

desc:
The plant life here is all brittle and twisted, finding little nutrients in the
parched ground. As you force through the dense undergrowth, thorny fingers grip
to your clothing and you can feel dry roots and branches breaking under your
weight. This dreary landscape goes on in all directions.
name: Scattered Brush

desc:
The land is evenly covered with dry grasses and bushy undergrowth striving to
draw nourishment from the dry soil. The brush gets denser looking north and east
while the terrain looks the same to the south. To the west the grass is the
dominant plant life.
name: Dry Land

desc:
Grass and small bushes dominate the landscape. Panning the horizon the terrain
looks the same to the north and south while to the north and east the landscape
is being taken over by bushes. This land has never been cultivated by civilised
hands.
name: Sparse Vegetation

desc:
Thin grasses that cover the ground like a carpet dominate the landscape with a
variety of other plants and small bushes striving to live here as well. To the
north the grass is giving room to more bushes while the terrain looks the same
in all other directions.
name: Sparse Vegetation

desc:
Low shrubbery scattered among the plains gives a little shelter from the wind,
but long grass seems to dominate the open landscape. Some small, thorny plants
that look like thistles grow in clusters among the grass. The plains continue in
all directions, but the patches of shrubbery seem taller in the north.
name: Sparse Vegetation

desc:
Other plants grow here but the grass is dominant and covers the ground in all
directions. The grass is rigid and makes movement tiresome. To the east the
grass slowly gives room for low bushes and other vegetation while the grass
grows even higher to the north.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
Looking around, the lush grassland extends out north and east as far as the eye
can see. The thriving grass covering the area is the tallest vegetation and is
only broken by patches of wild growing flowers. The plains continue to the
south. Farther into the distance, the familiar lines of the road to Tharbad can
be seen. A tall wooden building can be discerned in the northwest.
name: On a Rich Plateau

desc:
Lush green grass dominates this area leaving no room for bushes or trees. Small
patches are cultivated by the owner of the nearby farm. A tall wooden building
rises to the north with a wooden door facing this direction. Looking south the
familiar lines of the road to Tharbad can be seen and it easily accessible from
here.
name: Fields by the Road

desc:
Thick tall patches of grass grow over the soft, fertile ground that borders the
river here. North and south the narrow trail weaves its way along the riverbank
flowing south towards the Nen Kalthar. To the east lies a rectangular building,
it is a small farm building made with stone walls and a roof made from wood. A
small wooden door is facing in this direction.
name: Trail past a Small Farmhouse

desc:
A high riverbank sweeps down to the turbulent river on your west. From here, it
looks like the climb back up will be very difficult. The trail winds it way
north and south along the riverbank passing a small building on its way south.
The tall green grass is covering the ground except at the few places at the
riverbank where trees are taking advantage of the water supply.
name: Trail along the Riverbank

desc:
Overlooking a torrid and noisy river flowing southwards the riverbank appears
quite steep and treacherous along the edge, and indeed it would appear to be in
decline. The semblance of a narrow trail through the grass can be seen leading
north and south while open grassland spans across the eastern horizon.
name: Trail along the Riverbank

desc:
A steep riverbank quickly descends towards a fast flowing, turbulent river west
of the trail. A few trees have managed to root themselves in the riverbank but
there is very little other vegetation to hold the soil in place and it would be
quite hazardous to attempt the descent. East of the trail the rich grasslands
stretch as far as the eye can see.
name: Trail along the Riverbank

desc:
The ground is almost soggy making the various grasses thrive. The most plentiful
species has long, thick, bladelike leaves with a very distinct yellow colouring
at the tip. The plains seems to go on in all directions with no features of the
landscape breaking the monotony of the grass.
name: Damp Grassland

desc:
Going on in all directions the open country is covered with diverse grasses. The
most dominant kind has big, yellow leaves while others exhibit different shades
of red and tan. The soil is wet and almost mud-like giving ample nourishment to
the thriving grasses.
name: Damp Grassland

desc:
Long, slender grass covers the land almost as a giant green sea, bowing to the
air currents to maintain the illusion of water. There is almost no other plant
life managing to gain a foothold. The plains seem on go on in all directions
with no distinct terrain features to catch the eye.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The vast expanse of plains goes on unbroken by tree or other tall vegetation in
all directions except to the east where some bushes can be discerned. The most
dominating plant here by far is tall grass with broad, yellow leaves but other
grasses mix in.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The area is covered with patches of thorny gorse bushes. A few stands of grass
still hold their own ground but will eventually be strangled by the unforgiving
brush. The bushes grow denser to the east while they give room for other plants
to the south. To the west vast grasslands go as far as the horizon.
name: Edge of a Brush

desc:
Giving little room for other vegetation, dark-leaved green gorse bushes take up
most of the growing space. It creates a not impassable wall of thorns but any
movement further east will be difficult. A few patches of thin grass defiantly
struggle to keep alive at the foot of the smallest of the bushes but it is a
battle hard to win.
name: Withered Ground

desc:
Protected by large prickly thorns, the huge bushes and shrubs totally dominate
the landscape in all directions. Movement is difficult as the dense vegetation
leaves little room to manoeuvre. A few patches of short coarse grass manage to
thrive at the foot of the bushes but are ever in danger of being choked should
the bushes expand.
name: Deep in the Bushes

desc:
Like an impassable wall of thorns the big bushes grow all over with interwoven
branches and roots making movement very difficult. Even the leaves have pointed
thorns at the edges making them look sawlike. Very little other vegetation can
survive the dominance of the brush but a few strands of grass can be seen.
name: Deep in the Thorns

desc:
Strained and weary, the short, dry undergrowth cannot compete with the massive
gorse bushes that dominate the land. Armed with thousands of dangerous, needle
like thorns, the bushes threaten to leech all remaining life from the parched
ground. In a seemingly unrelenting conquest, the gorse grows higher and closer
together as they attempt to capture the land, yet their formation begins to
break in the west. Perhaps these bushes have only limited ambition.
name: Deep in the Thorns

desc:
An abundance of overgrown gorse bushes makes movement extremely difficult. They
try to tear at anything they can grab with their needle-like thorns. Passage
north and east is all but impossible, and the chance of going either south or
west seems even worse. The gorse has sucked all the life from the hard, dusty
ground that is void of any other vegetation.
name: Thick Gorse

desc:
Dry gorse bushes grow to a mans height all around the horizon making it hard to
move around. It is impossible to work your way farther to the north while it
might be possible to find a path in between the bushes farther east. Little
other vegetation grows here except the dry brown grass striving to survive.
name: Dense Brush

desc:
Tall dry bushes totally dominate the landscape only allowing short brown grass
to share what little nourishment the soil offers. The bushes have razor-sharp
leaves and stiff pointed thorns making them look extremely nasty. The bushes are
loosing space to the grass to the west and south while the brush goes on to the
north and west.
name: Scattered Brush

desc:
Tall green grass covers the ground everywhere outside the patches of bushes and
shrubbery. The bushes are getting more numerous to the north and east making
movement harder whereas they are getting more rare towards the south. The area
looks quite desolate with no signs of human presence.
name: Bushy Plains

desc:
Long lush grass creates a sea of green with wavelike movement created by the
wind. Dry bushes grow in large clumps looking like islands in the green ocean.
The land looks like this in all directions except to the south where the bushes
are notably smaller and grow farther apart.
name: Bushy Plains

desc:
Wading through the tall grass, long green blades wave hypnotically in the
breeze, accompanied by a gentle swishing sound. The sea of green continues west,
while scanning the horizon north and east, the terrain becomes more barren.
Large patches of bushes make dents in the green carpet.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
Long, spindly grass covers the ground nearly as far as the eye can see. A stout
wooden fence southwest of here marks the edge of a farm. The rich, fertile soil
offers plenty of nourishment for the local flora, and the grass might serve as
food for nearby fauna. A large plateau gently rises up to the west.
name: In the Long, Lush Grass

desc:
The flat ground is uninterrupted by bush or tree, giving a clear view of the
horizon. The tall grasses make room for wild flowers that splash the fields with
vivid colours and fill the air with a sweet perfume. The whole landscape looks
like a scene from a watercolour.
name: On a Rich Plateau

desc:
If surveying the surroundings, the extension of damp grasslands can be observed
north and south. The ground seems fertile but sports mostly grass and weeds, in
contrast to the plant life on the rich plateau east of here. A small wooden
building can be discerned to the southwest.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
The fertile ground leaks water as due pressure is applied, and old footprints
left by the natural inhabitants are well-preserved in the mud-like soil. East
and south the scenery seems much the same, but a short distance west the steady
sound of a fast flowing river can be heard. Tall grass is flourishing in the
waterladen soil.
name: Damp Grassland

desc:
Thick blades of grass, yellowing at the top, cover the saturated ground. The
footprints left on the ground by the natural inhabitants are filled with water
proving the ground to be extremely waterladen. Looking around, the grassland
extends to the west and north while to the south and east drier plains cover the
open landscape.
name: Damp Grassland

desc:
Studying the surrounding landscape, one can see plains sweeping out all across
the horizon, unbroken by tree or bush. The only sound to be heard is that of the
long, dry grass as it blows gently to-and-fro in the breeze. There is no telling
what kind of animals could be living here, hidden from the eye in the sea of
grass.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
Across the horizon, rich grassland spreads out north, west and south, while to
the east dense brush starts to take over control of the land. The grass becomes
taller towards the west while scattered gorse bushes are cluttering the scenery
towards the east.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
Tall patches of thin, healthy grass are strewn about amidst isolated stands of
thorny gorse bushes. Although the gorse owns the terrain to the north and east,
the bushes here are but young settlers. Their small, dark green leaves and short
thorns do little to compete with the established colonies of grasses and weeds.
Perhaps one day they will attempt a coup; but presently they just lie and wait.
name: Scattered Gorse

desc:
Dense but not impassable gorse covers the land, trying to snuff the struggling
grasses that grow defiantly at their feet. The proud bushes reach out towards
one another, displaying their thorns and darker green leaves. Yet the grasses
here are not as easily overcome as their allies to the northeast; they seem to
thrive despite the apparent adversity.
name: Reaching Bushes

desc:
As if responding to a cry for mercy, the taller, healthier grass from the west
surges in to rescue the withered native undergrowth. Clinging defiantly to the
broken soil while being choked by larger gorse bushes, the thin grass battles
for its right to live in this bitter landscape but the thorny bushes show no
mercy.
name: Dense Brush

desc:
Covered by coarse gorse bushes, the land has little hope of ever being released
from their iron grip. Short, parched brown grass grows in small clumps in spots
not yet conquered by the gorse bushes. Movement is difficult, since the prickly
thorns grab hold of anything they touch. The brush continues in all directions.
name: Dense Brush

desc:
Harsh moorland covered with thick, twisted bushes dominates the surrounding
landscape here, which offers little protection from the elements. Long grass
grows in between the bushes making the undergrowth a natural hideout for various
critters avoiding detection by predators.
name: At the Edge of the Brush

desc:
Beaten down by the strong winds, the dried grasses lie flat on the plains here.
Dense thickets of thorny bushes offer a little protection from the weather. The
loose grass and various twigs and branches that make up the terrain floor
provide perfect building materials for the birds that choose to nest here.
name: Coarse Grassland

desc:
Long dry grasses, combed to one side by the prevailing eastern wind, cover the
gently rolling landscape where the bush does not prevail. Looking around, there
is little sign of any distinguishing features that could act as a bearing, as
you move through this featureless landscape.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The plains stretch out north and east from here, undulating into the distance.
One can hear the occasional rustling sound as small animals run through the long
grass, trying to stay out of sight of any predators. West and south, the terrain
takes on a more fertile look.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The long green plains stretch out before you in all directions. Often the fair
stroke of a pleasant breeze blows across the plains and the soft scented grass
creates a relaxing ambience about the place. Wild strawberry bushes grow all
around, inviting you to have a taste.
name: On a Rich Plateau

desc:
A musty smell of hay, manure and rotting boards lingers throughout this small
wooden barn. With its southern door boarded shut, the building is fairly dark
and humid. It seems to get little ventilation from the wide, sliding barndoor on
the north wall, but it's enough to keep the stench bearable. Cobwebs cling to
the rafters of the low wooden ceiling above a couple of the pens, in spite of an
abundance of bird droppings.
name: Inside a Small Barn

desc:
A small pen hosts the farmer's cows keeping them safe from wolves at night.
Small patches of soil are used by the farmer for various crops including wheat
and vegetables. The patches make a nice pattern cutting squares in the tall
grass. To the south a tall wooden building can be seen, while a lower stone
building lies to the west.
name: Fields behind a Farmhouse

desc:
Built of smooth white stones speckled with brown and grey, this quaint little
structure seems to serve as a home to a small family. There is a wood-burning
stove standing tall in the centre of the room, possibly giving support to the
low wooden ceiling. Next to the stove, a table set for three waits to serve a
future meal, and three small beds line the northern wall, only a couple paces
farther away. A narrow glass window near the back door provides a glimpse of the
outside world, and several shelves and cabinets on the south wall appear to
obscure another window.
name: A Stone Farmhouse

desc:
A small, black cave lies beneath the ruins, the stairs from above bending so
sharply that this place never sees the light of day. The air is humid, and big
drops of water can be seen on the walls. To the east, the wall has at some time
collapsed, but it is difficult to tell what lies beyond. However, the air seems
stale and has a distinctly musty odour.
name: Slimy Cave

desc:
The walls and ceiling here are very wet, and moisture falls all around, pooling
in any small crack or indentation on the floor. A putrescent smell pervades the
whole chamber, and rotting flesh lies scattered all about, among the dust and
rubble.
name: Beyond the Brittle Crack

desc:
This hallway is the centre of the upper floor of this farmhouse. There are two
doorways, one is at the north end of the hall and the other is to the east. The
ceiling is made up of a series of boards placed next to each other. Some of the
boards seem to be older than the others.
name: A Long Hallway

desc:
This small room is cold but quite dry. The floor is made of hard-packed earth,
which has a slight, but not overpowering, musty odour. Judging by the neat row
of shelves along each wall, this place was obviously constructed with storage in
mind.
name: A Storage Cellar

desc:
This rustic bedroom is small, but well furnished. The small bed in one corner
seems to indicate that a young child sleeps in here. The large toy-chest, which
stands at the foot of the bed, also suggests that this is a child's room.
name: A Bedroom

desc:
This comparatively spacious chamber must be the bedroom of the owner of the
farm. Though simple in style, the furnishings are all well-made, hewn from the
finest oak. A large bed stands against one wall, beneath a curtained window, and
a copious wardrobe stands beside the door.
name: Master Bedroom

desc:
A strong smell of musty and stagnant air pierces your nostrils. The roof is
rather low, and anyone taller than a human child would need to stoop in this
place, to avoid a nasty knock on the head. Every part of the room is covered in
a thick and choking layer of dust.
name: In the Attic

desc:
This tiny room is obviously some sort of jail cell. It is dusty, and the floor
is littered with rubble. In the corner is a dark stain, but it is hard to tell
its exact nature. But the most overwhelming thing about this chamber is the foul
stench of death and decay.
name: A Prison Cell

desc:
A broken muddy trail runs alongside a fast flowing river, just west of here.
Delicate, slender trees grace the rivers edge and their long, slim branches
extend over the river below. It should be possible to scramble your way down the
steep banking into the river from here. Two buildings can be seen to the
northeast while a road runs by south of here.
name: Trail along the Riverbank

desc:
This bridge is located at a spot were the Nen Kalthar is rather narrow. It is a
high bridge - not too high to jump into the water, but certainly too high to
permit a traveller to climb upon it from the flowing waters below. Made from
blue-grey stones, this construction arches smoothly across the river, with a
wide, low parapet to either side, perfect for sitting and resting upon.
name: On a Small Stone Bridge

desc:
The bank here rises steeply on both sides, topped by a thin forest on the west.
You fancy that you can hear birdsong above the gentle sounds of the waters
bouncing its way down to the south, where it will ultimately gain its freedom in
the Great Sea. For the moment you can only follow the path of the fast- flowing
stream, with no possibility to ascend its steep and muddy banks.
name: A Fast-flowing Stream

desc:
South of here there is a well-built ford which looks like it is in good repair.
The fact that almost all of the stones remain intact is a tribute to the skill
and workmanship of the men who built it, for many years have passed since such
stonework has been carried out by the hands of men. The river here flows south,
joining another branch beyond the ford and then flowing onward, to the east.
name: A Tributary to the Nen Kalthar

desc:
The cold, damp earth floor of this hole partially covered in dead leaves and
small tufts of fur. The roof seems to be supported by a network of roots, but
nevertheless, soil and small stones fall to the ground, from time to time,
adding small flurries of dust to the foetid air.
name: In a Muddy Hole

desc:
Old dirt and dust is mixed with flour all over the place. Some big sacks stand
in a corner, next to a small table and chair. At the southwest corner of the
room, the broken and disused remains of an old pulley lifting system can be
seen, no longer used to transport sacks of flour between the mill above, and
this small storage room.
name: A Dusty Cellar
